decisions.text: Server-side tag extraction in `lib/tag-index.ts` — scans meeting files and returns structured index
decisions.meetingTitle: Architecture Review — Cross-Meeting Query System

decisions.text: Mtime-based cache file (`.council-tag-cache.json`) in the meetings directory — rebuild when any .md file is newer
decisions.meetingTitle: Architecture Review — Cross-Meeting Query System

decisions.text: Three API endpoints: `GET /api/meetings/tags` (query), `GET /api/meetings/tags/summary` (counts), `GET /api/meetings/unresolved` (carry-forward)
decisions.meetingTitle: Architecture Review — Cross-Meeting Query System

decisions.text: New MCP tool `council_query` for Claude Code to pull cross-meeting context
decisions.meetingTitle: Architecture Review — Cross-Meeting Query System

decisions.text: No file-watchers, no in-process cache — file-based cache works across all runtimes (Next.js, Electron, CLI)
decisions.meetingTitle: Architecture Review — Cross-Meeting Query System

decisions.text: Right-side 320px panel (not left, not inline filter)
decisions.meetingTitle: Design Review — Meeting Outcomes Sidebar Panel

decisions.text: New `MeetingOutcomes.tsx` component, separate from MeetingViewer
decisions.meetingTitle: Design Review — Meeting Outcomes Sidebar Panel

decisions.text: Regex extraction from raw markdown (`/(DECISION|OPEN|ACTION):\s*(.+)/gi`)
decisions.meetingTitle: Design Review — Meeting Outcomes Sidebar Panel

decisions.text: Toggle via "Outcomes (N)" pill in the stats bar
decisions.meetingTitle: Design Review — Meeting Outcomes Sidebar Panel

decisions.text: Grouped by type — Decisions first, Open Questions second, Actions third
decisions.meetingTitle: Design Review — Meeting Outcomes Sidebar Panel

decisions.text: Click-to-scroll via `scrollIntoView` with ID anchors on rendered blocks
decisions.meetingTitle: Design Review — Meeting Outcomes Sidebar Panel

decisions.text: Collapsed by default, content pushes left when open (desktop), overlay on tablet
decisions.meetingTitle: Design Review — Meeting Outcomes Sidebar Panel

decisions.text: Fix regex inconsistency first — `tag-index.ts` must match the bracket format (`[DECISION]`) that `md-components.tsx` already handles. This is a production correctness bug affecting every tag-dependent feature.
decisions.meetingTitle: Sprint Planning: Next Priorities

decisions.text: Real-data audit before synthetic test fixtures — run extraction against all existing meeting files and manually audit output. Synthetic tests come after, to cover the gaps the audit reveals.
decisions.meetingTitle: Sprint Planning: Next Priorities

decisions.text: Resolution tracking will use inline IDs — format `[OPEN:id]` / `[RESOLVED:id]` in meeting files. Preserves hub model, survives LLM paraphrasing, backward compatible (items without IDs remain visible but aren't auto-resolvable).
decisions.meetingTitle: Sprint Planning: Next Priorities

decisions.text: Scroll failure feedback will be inline on the outcomes panel item (not a toast) — muted label that fades after 3 seconds.
decisions.meetingTitle: Sprint Planning: Next Priorities

decisions.text: Drop participants field and synthetic test fixture from this sprint — participants field deferred to resolution tracking sprint; synthetic tests deferred until after real-data audit.
decisions.meetingTitle: Sprint Planning: Next Priorities

decisions.text: Legacy meeting indicator in status bar should be designed as a doorway, not a dead end — reserve space for a future "Generate outcomes" action even if it ships as copy-only now.
decisions.meetingTitle: Sprint Planning: Next Priorities

decisions.text: Use inline tagging (`DECISION:`, `OPEN:`, `ACTION:`) instead of post-hoc parsing. The facilitator tags during generation, keeping the meeting file as single source of truth.
decisions.meetingTitle: Strategy Session — Making Meeting Outcomes Actionable

decisions.text: No action item tracking system. Surface action items but don't own their lifecycle.
decisions.meetingTitle: Strategy Session — Making Meeting Outcomes Actionable

decisions.text: No database, no sidecar files. All structured data derived on-demand from meeting files.
decisions.meetingTitle: Strategy Session — Making Meeting Outcomes Actionable

decisions.text: Decisions are the highest-value output. Prioritize over action items or open questions.
decisions.meetingTitle: Strategy Session — Making Meeting Outcomes Actionable

decisions.text: **Primary user action:** Agent Council enables "maintaining continuity across meetings." The concrete implementation hierarchy is: (1) live meetings, (2) recent completions, (3) planned/next, (4) historical cross-meeting data. This ordering governs page layout and feature priority.
decisions.meetingTitle: Design Review: Meetings Page & Discovery UX

decisions.text: **Remove aggregated suggestions from the list view.** Suggestions should not be collected into a growing list on the meetings page. Instead, they remain attached to their source meeting and are surfaced in a completion card in the detail view. This eliminates the accumulation problem entirely -- there is nothing to cap, expire, or deduplicate.
decisions.meetingTitle: Design Review: Meetings Page & Discovery UX

decisions.text: **Add a completion card to the detail view.** When a meeting completes, a distinct card appears at the bottom of the content showing: key decisions (max 3), open questions needing follow-up (max 2), suggested next meetings (max 2) with single-click "+ Plan" buttons, and a link to the full Outcomes panel. The card should be visually distinct from the meeting content -- bordered, elevated, action-oriented.
decisions.meetingTitle: Design Review: Meetings Page & Discovery UX

decisions.text: **Move the cross-meeting status bar below the meeting list.** It is reference material, not primary navigation. Its data quality (especially open questions, which lack RESOLVED tracking) does not yet justify a top-of-page position.
decisions.meetingTitle: Design Review: Meetings Page & Discovery UX

decisions.text: **Window the status bar to recent meetings.** Open questions and actions should display only from the last 5 meetings, not all meetings. Decisions remain cumulative. This prevents the "numbers only go up" problem while RESOLVED tagging matures.
decisions.meetingTitle: Design Review: Meetings Page & Discovery UX

decisions.text: **Do not implement time-based expiration for suggested meetings.** Relevance is not correlated with recency.
decisions.meetingTitle: Design Review: Meetings Page & Discovery UX

decisions.text: **New page hierarchy for the list view:** (1) Title + "Plan meeting" button, (2) Live meeting card (promoted, distinct styling), (3) Planned meetings / "Up Next" section, (4) Search + filter (inline), (5) Meeting cards, (6) Cross-meeting status bar (bottom).
decisions.meetingTitle: Design Review: Meetings Page & Discovery UX

decisions.text: **Build dismiss, skip everything else.** The suggestion system does not yet warrant lifecycle tooling beyond a simple dismiss mechanism. The investment is proportional to the feature's uncertain value: ~3.5 hours total for dismiss + structured data + template fix. No auto-resolve, no consolidation, no backlog review agents, no persistent suggestion index.
decisions.meetingTitle: Strategy Session: Suggested Meetings Lifecycle & Backlog Management

decisions.text: **Keep suggestions and planned meetings as separate systems.** The semantic boundary between "system-recommended" (suggestions) and "user-committed" (planned meetings) is meaningful. The promotion path (`+ Queue`) is the intentional bridge. Do not merge the data models.
decisions.meetingTitle: Strategy Session: Suggested Meetings Lifecycle & Backlog Management

decisions.text: **Enrich `recommendedMeetings` from `string[]` to `{ text: string, type?: string, topic?: string }[]`.** Parse the existing "Type: topic" convention in suggestion text into structured fields. This is a ~45-minute change that enables better display and cleaner dismiss hashing, without requiring a new data model.
decisions.meetingTitle: Strategy Session: Suggested Meetings Lifecycle & Backlog Management

decisions.text: **Store dismiss state in `.council-suggestion-state.json`.** A dedicated file with entries containing hash, sourceMeeting, text, status, and dismissedAt. Separate from planned meetings. Append-only invariant: the file reflects suggestions as they existed when first parsed.
decisions.meetingTitle: Strategy Session: Suggested Meetings Lifecycle & Backlog Management

decisions.text: **Fix the source, not just the sink.** Update the facilitator template to make recommended follow-ups exceptional rather than default. Specific guidance: "Do not recommend follow-ups as a default. Most meetings resolve their own threads. If no follow-up is needed, omit this section entirely."
decisions.meetingTitle: Strategy Session: Suggested Meetings Lifecycle & Backlog Management

decisions.text: **Structured meeting outputs via JSON appendix (Architect's Option B).** The facilitator will produce a machine-readable JSON block wrapped in `<!-- meeting-outcomes -->` HTML comments at the end of every meeting, in addition to the existing markdown summary. Schema includes `schema_version`, `decisions`, `actions`, and `open_questions` arrays. This replaces reliance on regex tag extraction as the primary structured data source. Rationale: regex extraction has known reliability issues (open question from prior meetings); structured source data eliminates ambiguity.
decisions.meetingTitle: Strategy Session: Development Workflows & Agent Evolution

decisions.text: **Agent context files, narrow scope, meeting-derived.** Each agent gets a `*.context.md` file alongside its template in `.claude/agents/`. The facilitator updates these after each meeting with relevant learnings (estimates, recommendations, outcomes). Scope is intentionally narrow: only meeting-derived facts. Sections for Project Conventions and Domain Knowledge are reserved as placeholders. Rationale: this is architecturally simple (file concatenation), automatically maintained (facilitator writes it), and has clear provenance.
decisions.meetingTitle: Strategy Session: Development Workflows & Agent Evolution

decisions.text: **Deepen existing agents, don't widen the roster.** No new agents this cycle. Instead: add a "Verification & Testability" lens to the critic template (covering test plans, error states, rollback plans, production metrics). If a genuinely missing perspective surfaces during the validation experiment that can't be covered by lenses or context, the team will revisit. Rationale: each additional agent increases meeting time and token cost; composable lenses achieve specialization without these costs.
decisions.meetingTitle: Strategy Session: Development Workflows & Agent Evolution

decisions.text: **Validate with a 5-meeting experiment before further investment.** Run 5 meetings on real development topics using structured outputs. Track three success criteria: (1) at least 60% of ACTION items become committed code within 1 week, (2) at least 3 of 5 meetings surface an insight that wouldn't have emerged from a solo session, (3) JSON appendix is parseable without regex fallback in at least 4 of 5 meetings. Rationale: we have no external usage validation; dogfooding with measurable criteria is the minimum viable validation.
decisions.meetingTitle: Strategy Session: Development Workflows & Agent Evolution

decisions.text: **Triage existing backlog before starting the experiment.** The 23 pending action items and 20 open questions from prior meetings must be triaged (close stale items, consolidate duplicates, identify the 5 that matter) before the validation experiment begins. Rationale: testing a new system while the old backlog is unresolved would confound the results.
decisions.meetingTitle: Strategy Session: Development Workflows & Agent Evolution

decisions.text: Split MeetingViewer.tsx into 5 files: thin router, MeetingList.tsx, MeetingDetail.tsx, useMeetingData.ts, useProjectContext.ts — all co-located in app/meetings/ — Natural seam at if(!selected) branch, 2354 lines with 88 hooks is unmaintainable
decisions.meetingTitle: Design Review: MeetingViewer.tsx Component Architecture Split

decisions.text: Use custom hook approach for shared state — useMeetingData(activeProject) for polling/state, useProjectContext() for fetch-once project state — Minimum viable architectural change. No new dependencies. Context and useReducer are overkill for 2 consumers.
decisions.meetingTitle: Design Review: MeetingViewer.tsx Component Architecture Split

decisions.text: Pass activeProject as a string primitive to useMeetingData, not as an object — Avoids memoization footgun and unnecessary re-renders from object reference changes
decisions.meetingTitle: Design Review: MeetingViewer.tsx Component Architecture Split

decisions.text: MeetingDetail receives an onBack callback prop rather than calling selectMeeting(null) directly — Enables future composability — dashboard embedding, notification panels — without coupling to list-detail pattern
decisions.meetingTitle: Design Review: MeetingViewer.tsx Component Architecture Split

decisions.text: Move getContentForRound() to lib/meeting-utils.ts — Pure string processing, no React dependency, belongs in utils
decisions.meetingTitle: Design Review: MeetingViewer.tsx Component Architecture Split

decisions.text: The split PR must be a mechanical move only — zero behavioral changes — Mixing refactoring and behavior changes is the #1 cause of subtle regressions in large file splits
decisions.meetingTitle: Design Review: MeetingViewer.tsx Component Architecture Split

decisions.text: 6-scenario manual verification required before merge — No automated tests exist for MeetingViewer; manual verification is the safety net
decisions.meetingTitle: Design Review: MeetingViewer.tsx Component Architecture Split

decisions.text: Fix the tag index cache-bust after `council_resolve_question` — this is a bug, not a feature. Add `invalidateTagCache()` to `lib/tag-index.ts` and call it from the resolve API route after successful append.
decisions.meetingTitle: Design Review: MCP Read-Write Architecture — Making the Collaboration Loop Work

decisions.text: The current 12-tool MCP surface is correct. No new tools until evidence from meetings 3-5 demonstrates a gap.
decisions.meetingTitle: Design Review: MCP Read-Write Architecture — Making the Collaboration Loop Work

decisions.text: Add capped activity logging (`.council-activity.jsonl`, max 200 entries, truncate oldest on write) to the 4 write tool handlers in `mcp/server.mjs`. No UI for now. Checkpoint at meeting 5: if nothing reads the log, delete the logging code.
decisions.meetingTitle: Design Review: MCP Read-Write Architecture — Making the Collaboration Loop Work

decisions.text: Context persistence (making `council_add_context` write to disk) is deferred. No evidence of demand. Ephemeral context is acceptable for now.
decisions.meetingTitle: Design Review: MCP Read-Write Architecture — Making the Collaboration Loop Work

decisions.text: The viewer-to-Claude communication model is async intent-queueing. This is architecturally correct and should not change. "Start meeting" buttons, suggestion approvals, and future intent channels all follow this pattern.
decisions.meetingTitle: Design Review: MCP Read-Write Architecture — Making the Collaboration Loop Work

decisions.text: The three-tier persistence model (ephemeral / session-file / canonical) is adopted as the conceptual framework for future persistence decisions, even though only tiers 1 and 3 are implemented today. Session tier uses append-only JSONL when needed.
decisions.meetingTitle: Design Review: MCP Read-Write Architecture — Making the Collaboration Loop Work

decisions.text: Hardening sprint with zero new features — 30 commits of features without tests or structural fixes has created risk
decisions.meetingTitle: Sprint Planning: What Should We Actually Build Next?

decisions.text: Two-PR hook split: Option A (mechanical split) then Option B (boundary fix), committed as single sprint item — Staged approach gives rollback checkpoint while ensuring full architectural fix happens
decisions.meetingTitle: Sprint Planning: What Should We Actually Build Next?

decisions.text: Connection health gating bundled into PR 1 using shared ref (not state) — 5 independent polling intervals need coordinated backoff when server is down
decisions.meetingTitle: Sprint Planning: What Should We Actually Build Next?

decisions.text: Template version check uses hash-based body comparison — Simpler than version numbering, works retroactively for existing installations
decisions.meetingTitle: Sprint Planning: What Should We Actually Build Next?

decisions.text: Explicit exclusion list: no agents/page.tsx split, no new features, no tests, no suggestion lifecycle, no telemetry, no new MCP tools — Prevent scope creep in a hardening sprint
decisions.meetingTitle: Sprint Planning: What Should We Actually Build Next?

decisions.text: The validation experiment criteria are assessed honestly: Criterion 1 (ACTION-to-code) at 36% actuals but on trajectory to pass; Criterion 2 (insight emergence) passes at 3 genuine insights across 4 meetings; Criterion 3 (JSON parseability) passes at 3/3 with this meeting making 4/4. Overall: the experiment demonstrates real but modest value.
decisions.meetingTitle: Retrospective: Are We Building the Right Thing?

decisions.text: Open questions that have carried forward through 3+ meetings without progress must be archived with a trigger condition, not silently carried forward. This prevents the carry-forward list from becoming a graveyard.
decisions.meetingTitle: Retrospective: Are We Building the Right Thing?

decisions.text: Meeting 5 will be a Strategy Session titled "Operational Cadence for Agent Council." It must produce a one-page operating protocol covering: meeting frequency, format selection criteria (decision-complexity rubric), mandatory practices, and revised success measurement criteria.
decisions.meetingTitle: Retrospective: Are We Building the Right Thing?

decisions.text: The hook split sprint from Meeting 3 proceeds as designed. No additional meetings needed to start execution.
decisions.meetingTitle: Retrospective: Are We Building the Right Thing?

decisions.text: Meeting 5 must include revised validation criteria for future experiments, replacing the current three criteria which were a first draft.
decisions.meetingTitle: Retrospective: Are We Building the Right Thing?

decisions.text: The primary value of meetings is constraint-setting (saying NO), not action-item generation. This reframes how meetings should be designed and evaluated.
decisions.meetingTitle: Retrospective: Are We Building the Right Thing?

decisions.text: Meetings are event-driven, not calendar-driven. No standing meetings. Standups at start of work sessions; full meetings only when a decision has multiple viable paths and cannot be resolved solo.
decisions.meetingTitle: Strategy Session: Operational Cadence -- One-Page Operating Protocol

decisions.text: Three-tier meeting model adopted: Tier 0 (Direction Check, 1 round, 2 agents), Tier 1 (Quick Consult, 1 round, 3 agents), Tier 2 (Full Meeting, 2-3 rounds, 4-5 agents). The facilitator selects the tier based on decision complexity.
decisions.meetingTitle: Strategy Session: Operational Cadence -- One-Page Operating Protocol

decisions.text: Technical full meetings require agents to read actual source files before contributing. Non-technical meetings do not require code-reading.
decisions.meetingTitle: Strategy Session: Operational Cadence -- One-Page Operating Protocol

decisions.text: The facilitator decides meeting weight, not the requester. The requester describes the problem; the facilitator selects the format.
decisions.meetingTitle: Strategy Session: Operational Cadence -- One-Page Operating Protocol

decisions.text: Max 2 full meetings (Tier 2) per week. Standups and quick consults are uncapped.
decisions.meetingTitle: Strategy Session: Operational Cadence -- One-Page Operating Protocol

decisions.text: Retrospective trigger is advisory: consider a retrospective after 50+ commits or 2 weeks. Skip if no specific question to examine.
decisions.meetingTitle: Strategy Session: Operational Cadence -- One-Page Operating Protocol

decisions.text: Revised validation criteria replace the original three: (1) decision durability -- 80%+ unchanged after 30 days, (2) constraint yield -- 1+ "do not" constraint per full meeting.
decisions.meetingTitle: Strategy Session: Operational Cadence -- One-Page Operating Protocol

decisions.text: The validation experiment concludes with the operating protocol as its primary deliverable. Experiment scored 2/3 on original criteria (Criterion 1 failed at 36% actuals, Criteria 2 and 3 passed).
decisions.meetingTitle: Strategy Session: Operational Cadence -- One-Page Operating Protocol

decisions.text: All 5 agents unanimously accepted the PM's final protocol text as the operating protocol for Agent Council.
decisions.meetingTitle: Strategy Session: Operational Cadence -- One-Page Operating Protocol

decisions.text: **Option 3 (MCP bridge), scoped incrementally.** Agent Council stays a viewer — it never spawns or manages code execution. Instead, it exposes action items through MCP so that Claude Code sessions can pull work items on demand. This respects the architectural boundary (Council observes, Claude Code executes) and builds on infrastructure that already exists (tag indexer, MCP server).
decisions.meetingTitle: Quick Consult: Execution Bridge — Connecting Decisions to Implementation

decisions.text: Agent Council is an AI-first product. Meeting transcripts optimize for AI context richness. Human-facing outputs (summaries, digests, session briefs) optimize for human scannability and engagement. The primary consumer of meeting content is Claude Code (AI agents in future sessions), not human developers reading transcripts.
decisions.meetingTitle: Design Review: Agent Council as a Product — What Features Make This Genuinely Useful?

decisions.text: The architecture should grow the bridge (MCP intelligence layer) rather than the viewer (web app features). The viewer is feature-complete. Future investment goes into smarter MCP tools and session continuity.
decisions.meetingTitle: Design Review: Agent Council as a Product — What Features Make This Genuinely Useful?

decisions.text: Agent Council is an enhancement to Claude Code, not a dependency. All MCP tools must degrade gracefully when the viewer is not running — returning helpful fallback messages, never errors.
decisions.meetingTitle: Design Review: Agent Council as a Product — What Features Make This Genuinely Useful?

decisions.text: Start with filtering the roadmap by the JSON appendix priority field before building a separate work items data store. The promoted work item entity is architecturally sound but premature — filter first, observe whether more is needed.
decisions.meetingTitle: Design Review: Agent Council as a Product — What Features Make This Genuinely Useful?

decisions.text: The session brief is AI-optimized (rich, structured, detailed). The digest copy is human-optimized (brief, scannable, actionable). These are two different outputs from the same meeting data, serving different consumers.
decisions.meetingTitle: Design Review: Agent Council as a Product — What Features Make This Genuinely Useful?

decisions.text: Facilitator updates its own context file post-meeting with key learnings. Individual agent context files are updated manually or in dedicated maintenance sessions, not automatically after every meeting.
decisions.meetingTitle: Design Review: Agent Council as a Product — What Features Make This Genuinely Useful?

open.text: Should the cache also index meeting metadata (title, date, participants) for richer queries?
open.meetingTitle: Architecture Review — Cross-Meeting Query System

open.text: How does the facilitator know which OPEN items have been resolved in subsequent meetings vs just not mentioned?
open.meetingTitle: Architecture Review — Cross-Meeting Query System

open.text: How reliable is start-of-line regex matching against real LLM-generated meeting content?
open.meetingTitle: Design Review — Meeting Outcomes Sidebar Panel

open.text: Should the panel show a confidence indicator for extraction quality?
open.meetingTitle: Design Review — Meeting Outcomes Sidebar Panel

open.text: What's the failure UX when a scroll anchor can't find its target?
open.meetingTitle: Design Review — Meeting Outcomes Sidebar Panel

open.text: How do we make ID generation reliable for facilitator agents? Options: (a) IDs auto-assigned by the viewer, (b) strict template requirement, (c) IDs optional — only items with IDs are auto-resolvable. Designer preference: option (c).
open.meetingTitle: Sprint Planning: Next Priorities

open.text: If the sprint is time-boxed to one session (~6 hours real time), which items are explicitly dropped? The 9-item list at full scope is 8-10 hours, not 5-6. Team should decide now rather than discover it at hour 5.
open.meetingTitle: Sprint Planning: Next Priorities

open.text: What is the current state of the Electron build? Is `.council-tag-cache.json` path resolution correct in the packaged app?
open.meetingTitle: Sprint Planning: Next Priorities

open.text: What is the primary user action we want Agent Council to make easier? "Understand past decisions" vs "run better meetings" — these imply different priority orderings.
open.meetingTitle: Sprint Planning: Next Priorities

open.text: How reliable will LLM-generated tags be? Will facilitators consistently apply them?
open.meetingTitle: Strategy Session — Making Meeting Outcomes Actionable

open.text: What's the UX for meetings that predate the tagging system?
open.meetingTitle: Strategy Session — Making Meeting Outcomes Actionable

open.text: Should carry-forward prompts be automatic or opt-in per meeting?
open.meetingTitle: Strategy Session — Making Meeting Outcomes Actionable

open.text: Should suggestions and planned meetings share a data model, or remain separate systems? Currently planned meetings are server-side (JSON file) and suggestions are client-side (computed from meeting data). The completion card approach reduces urgency here since suggestions no longer need persistent state on the list view.
open.meetingTitle: Design Review: Meetings Page & Discovery UX

open.text: Should the long-term vision for the status bar be a natural-language briefing rather than counts? e.g., "3 open questions from the last 2 days. 1 action pending from yesterday's sprint planning." This would require templated string generation from the tag index. Not for v1, but worth keeping on record.
open.meetingTitle: Design Review: Meetings Page & Discovery UX

open.text: The 4 concurrent polling intervals (2s, 5s, 10s, 30s) should be audited and potentially consolidated into a shared polling hook during the component split.
open.meetingTitle: Design Review: Meetings Page & Discovery UX

open.text: The empty state does not distinguish between "never used the app" and "used the app, nothing is live right now." Not urgent, but a gap in the returning-user experience.
open.meetingTitle: Design Review: Meetings Page & Discovery UX

open.text: If the user insists on an aggregated suggestion view after seeing the dismiss-in-detail-view approach, should it be a tab/panel on the meetings page or a separate route? Do not build unless requested.
open.meetingTitle: Strategy Session: Suggested Meetings Lifecycle & Backlog Management

open.text: Should the facilitator eventually manage suggestion lifecycle during carry-forward (reading pending suggestions, judging whether they have been implicitly resolved, and updating statuses)? This is architecturally sound but premature. The `.council-suggestion-state.json` file supports this future without changes.
open.meetingTitle: Strategy Session: Suggested Meetings Lifecycle & Backlog Management

open.text: When should we evaluate whether suggestions are earning their keep? A concrete checkpoint (e.g., after 20 meetings or 1 month of use) should be set to revisit this strategy.
open.meetingTitle: Strategy Session: Suggested Meetings Lifecycle & Backlog Management

open.text: Should we treat OPEN item resolution (via RESOLVED tags) as the pilot for lifecycle patterns, deferring suggestion lifecycle learnings until we see whether loop-closing works in practice for the simpler case?
open.meetingTitle: Strategy Session: Suggested Meetings Lifecycle & Backlog Management

open.text: What is the retention policy for agent context files? The critic raised that without a maximum length or rolling window, context files will grow until they cause prompt-length problems. No policy was decided -- this needs resolution before context files accumulate significant content.
open.meetingTitle: Strategy Session: Development Workflows & Agent Evolution

open.text: Beyond facilitator post-meeting updates, what other triggers should update agent context files? Manual edits? Automated detection of estimate accuracy? This was noted as a future concern but not resolved.
open.meetingTitle: Strategy Session: Development Workflows & Agent Evolution

open.text: Should Agent Council support lightweight, single-agent consultations outside of full meetings? The north-star raised this as a future capability. Not designed, not scoped, but flagged as a direction to keep open architecturally.
open.meetingTitle: Strategy Session: Development Workflows & Agent Evolution

open.text: We have no usage telemetry. We don't know which features are used, how often meetings are run, or whether the viewer provides value beyond meeting files. The PM flagged this but it was explicitly descoped from the current plan. It remains a gap for prioritization decisions.
open.meetingTitle: Strategy Session: Development Workflows & Agent Evolution

open.text: Is Agent Council's value proposition "better meetings" or "decisions that stick"? The north-star proposed the latter. This framing was not formally debated but has implications for positioning, documentation, and feature prioritization.
open.meetingTitle: Strategy Session: Development Workflows & Agent Evolution

open.text: Where exactly does selectMeeting() live — in the router or in useMeetingData? It uses useRouter for URL sync.
open.meetingTitle: Design Review: MeetingViewer.tsx Component Architecture Split

open.text: When should Claude poll each intent queue (planned meetings, suggestions, input)? During meetings only? At session start? Between meetings? This needs a documented convention in the facilitator template.
open.meetingTitle: Design Review: MCP Read-Write Architecture — Making the Collaboration Loop Work

open.text: Should a future MCP tool produce synthesized project health summaries (executive briefings) rather than raw query results? Flagged as north-star direction, not for current scope.
open.meetingTitle: Design Review: MCP Read-Write Architecture — Making the Collaboration Loop Work

open.text: (Carry-forward) Retention policy for agent context files still unresolved. Now also applies to `.council-activity.jsonl`.
open.meetingTitle: Design Review: MCP Read-Write Architecture — Making the Collaboration Loop Work

open.text: How should action item completion work? Identification (hashing is fragile), verification (who confirms?), and collaborative actions (assigned to roles, not Claude) need design before a tool is built.
open.meetingTitle: Design Review: MCP Read-Write Architecture — Making the Collaboration Loop Work

open.text: Which recent features (word cloud, timeline, contribution chart, RSS) actually serve the core use case? Cannot answer without telemetry.
open.meetingTitle: Sprint Planning: What Should We Actually Build Next?

open.text: When do we add tests? North-star proposes sprint N+1 with 4 targeted integration tests.
open.meetingTitle: Sprint Planning: What Should We Actually Build Next?

open.text: How to handle template upgrades for projects with customized facilitator files?
open.meetingTitle: Sprint Planning: What Should We Actually Build Next?

open.text: How do we prevent the open-questions list from becoming noise? The 3-meeting archival rule addresses carry-forward, but does not address the rate of new questions per meeting.
open.meetingTitle: Retrospective: Are We Building the Right Thing?

open.text: Meeting action items have no path to tracked work items outside the meeting system. They depend on someone remembering to do them. The causal link between "meeting decided X" and "X got done" is unclear.
open.meetingTitle: Retrospective: Are We Building the Right Thing?

open.text: Should Agent Council support per-meeting roster selection beyond the mandatory triad? No mechanism currently exists for the user to say "include designer, exclude architect for this meeting."
open.meetingTitle: Retrospective: Are We Building the Right Thing?

open.text: What is the resolution rate for open questions? If below 20% per meeting, the carry-forward system produces debt, not value.
open.meetingTitle: Retrospective: Are We Building the Right Thing?

open.text: How does the facilitator agent's system prompt reference the CLAUDE.md protocol? The protocol is only useful if the facilitator operationalizes it.
open.meetingTitle: Strategy Session: Operational Cadence -- One-Page Operating Protocol

open.text: Does this protocol work for projects other than Agent Council? True validation requires testing on a different codebase.
open.meetingTitle: Strategy Session: Operational Cadence -- One-Page Operating Protocol

open.text: What is the achievable target time-to-value for a new user? Current estimate is 10-15 minutes. The plan aims for 2-3 minutes with demo meeting + MCP auto-config. Needs validation with an external user.
open.meetingTitle: Design Review: Agent Council as a Product — What Features Make This Genuinely Useful?

open.text: Should Agent Council position itself as a session continuity tool (broader than meetings) or stay focused on the meeting companion niche? The north-star advocates for the broader framing ("memory layer for AI-assisted development"). This was directionally endorsed but not formally scoped.
open.meetingTitle: Design Review: Agent Council as a Product — What Features Make This Genuinely Useful?

open.text: MCP auto-configuration needs testing across Windows, Mac, and Linux. File paths and permissions differ. Edge cases around invalid existing configs, missing directories, and concurrent writes need enumeration before building.
open.meetingTitle: Design Review: Agent Council as a Product — What Features Make This Genuinely Useful?

actions.text: Build MeetingOutcomes.tsx with regex extraction and scroll anchors
actions.meetingTitle: Design Review — Meeting Outcomes Sidebar Panel

actions.text: **Split MeetingViewer.tsx** into MeetingList.tsx, MeetingDetail.tsx, useMeetingPolling.ts (shared hook), and a thin MeetingViewer.tsx router. Do this first -- it is a prerequisite for the other changes. Estimated: 2 hours.
actions.meetingTitle: Design Review: Meetings Page & Discovery UX

actions.text: **Remove the aggregated suggestions collapsible** from the list view (current lines 841-906 in MeetingViewer.tsx). This is a deletion, not a refactor.
actions.meetingTitle: Design Review: Meetings Page & Discovery UX

actions.text: **Build the completion card component** for the detail view. Shows when `detail.status === 'complete'`. Contains: up to 3 decisions, up to 2 open questions, up to 2 suggested next meetings with "+ Plan" buttons, link to Outcomes panel, and "Back to meetings" navigation. Visually distinct from meeting content.
actions.meetingTitle: Design Review: Meetings Page & Discovery UX

actions.text: **Reorder the list view** to match the new hierarchy: live meetings promoted to top, planned meetings below that, search + filters inline, meeting cards, status bar at bottom.
actions.meetingTitle: Design Review: Meetings Page & Discovery UX

actions.text: **Window the status bar** to show open questions and actions from the last 5 meetings only. Decisions remain cumulative. Update the tag summary API or client-side filtering accordingly.
actions.meetingTitle: Design Review: Meetings Page & Discovery UX

actions.text: **Update the facilitator template** to make Recommended Follow-ups exceptional, not default. Include guidance: "Do not recommend follow-ups as a default. Most meetings resolve their own threads. If no follow-up is needed, omit this section entirely." -- facilitator template (~15 min)
actions.meetingTitle: Strategy Session: Suggested Meetings Lifecycle & Backlog Management

actions.text: **Enrich suggestion parsing** in `app/api/meetings/route.ts` to extract type and topic from existing free-text suggestions. Update `recommendedMeetings` type in `lib/types.ts` from `string[]` to structured objects. -- developer (~45 min)
actions.meetingTitle: Strategy Session: Suggested Meetings Lifecycle & Backlog Management

actions.text: **Build suggestion dismiss** -- create `.council-suggestion-state.json`, new API endpoint at `/api/council/meeting-suggestions`, dismiss button in the completion card UI. Hash is computed from sourceMeeting + original text. -- developer (~2 hours)
actions.meetingTitle: Strategy Session: Suggested Meetings Lifecycle & Backlog Management

actions.text: **Update MeetingViewer.tsx** to consume the enriched `recommendedMeetings` format and filter out dismissed suggestions before rendering. -- developer (~30 min)
actions.meetingTitle: Strategy Session: Suggested Meetings Lifecycle & Backlog Management

actions.text: Build JSON appendix parser in `lib/tag-index.ts` with fallback to regex extraction and forward-compatible field handling -- assigned to developer. Estimated effort: 3-4 hours.

actions.meetingTitle: Strategy Session: Development Workflows & Agent Evolution

actions.meetingTitle: Design Review: MCP Read-Write Architecture — Making the Collaboration Loop Work

actions.text: Add capped activity logging to 4 MCP write tools in `mcp/server.mjs`, writing to `.council-activity.jsonl` with 200-entry cap (truncate on write) — assigned to developer. 30 minutes.
actions.meetingTitle: Design Review: MCP Read-Write Architecture — Making the Collaboration Loop Work

actions.text: Between meetings 3 and 4, manually test the between-meetings MCP loop: use `council_add_context`, `council_resolve_question`, and `council_schedule_meeting` from a non-facilitator session. Document what was attempted and what was useful in the meeting 4 context section — assigned to facilitator.
actions.meetingTitle: Design Review: MCP Read-Write Architecture — Making the Collaboration Loop Work

actions.text: At meeting 5, review the activity log. If nothing has consumed the data, delete the logging code and the log file — assigned to project-manager.
actions.meetingTitle: Design Review: MCP Read-Write Architecture — Making the Collaboration Loop Work

actions.text: Triage the 33 open questions using the 3-meeting carry-forward rule: archive stale questions with trigger conditions, resolve silently-resolved ones, keep only active questions. -- assigned to project-manager. Effort: 30 minutes.

actions.text: After shipping onboarding fixes, prioritize the session brief as the next milestone — this is the feature that turns Agent Council from a novelty into a daily tool. — assigned to project-manager
